purpose: Map NSW Stage 6 mathematics syllabus nodes from the 2017 syllabuses to the 2024 11-12 syllabuses for future question categorisation and cross-syllabus lookup.
sourceSummary: Built from official NSW Government 2017 syllabus pages and DOCX downloads, and official NSW Curriculum 2024 overview/content pages. The app corpus includes displayable Advanced, Standard, Extension 1, and Extension 2 syllabus nodes; future courses can still be staged as conversion-only mappings until their corpora are added.

implementation.replacementNotes: The 2024 syllabus starts Year 11 implementation in 2026, Year 12 implementation in Term 4 2026, with the first HSC exam in 2027.

contentGroups.title: Reciprocal trigonometric functions, identities, expressions and equations

Probability, Venn diagrams and discrete probability distributions
Function transformations, graph sketching, asymptotes, inequalities and graphical solutions
Transformations, graphs, equations and modelling with trigonometric functions
Differentiation of trigonometric, exponential and logarithmic functions
Financial applications of sequences and series

nodes.title: Descriptive Statistics and Bivariate Data Analysis

contentGroups.title: Data (grouped and ungrouped) and summary statistics
Properties of functions, relations and graphs
Trigonometry with angles of any magnitude
Reciprocal trigonometric functions, identities, expressions and equations
The derivative as a rate of change
Function transformations, graphing circles, and order of transformations

mappings.notes: Closest direct match. The 2024 node expands and separates some old F1 function and relation work into more explicit content groups.
Use for questions involving function transformations, circles, discontinuities, or graph behaviour beyond basic function notation.
Direct match for acute-angle trigonometry, any-magnitude angles, and radians.
The 2024 focus-area title names equations explicitly; the core identity/equation content remains aligned.
Direct Year 11 calculus match, with 2024 making rate-of-change and graphical derivative applications more explicit.
Direct match for exponentials, logarithms, laws, equations, and graphs.
Use for applied logarithmic-scale modelling questions. Do not use for routine exponential/logarithmic algebra unless the 2024 Year 11 node is the better match.
Probability and conditional probability stay in Year 11. The discrete random variable part of old S1 maps to 2024 Year 12 Random variables.
Use this for questions involving discrete random variables or probability distributions rather than basic probability rules.
Most old Year 12 Graphing Techniques content is now a Year 11 Graph transformations focus area.
Use when the question is a modelling problem with functions or transformations rather than routine sketching.
Old trigonometric graphs and periodic modelling are now in the Year 12 functions focus area rather than a standalone Trigonometric functions Year 12 node.
Direct match for differentiation methods, including trigonometric, exponential and logarithmic functions.
The 2024 node broadens the title from applications of differentiation to applications of calculus, but the question-level mapping is usually direct.
Direct match for primitives, definite integrals, and area. 2024 also separates function-family integration groups.
Use these 2024 content groups when a question explicitly integrates exponential, logarithmic, or trigonometric functions.
Financial applications remain financial mathematics. Sequence/series mechanics are now a separate 2024 area of study.
Arithmetic and geometric sequences/series have moved from old Financial Mathematics into a standalone 2024 area of study.
Use for grouped/ungrouped data and summary statistics. Bivariate data and regression should be flagged for review because it is not a standalone 2024 Advanced focus area.

Bivariate analysis has no clear like-for-like 2024 Advanced focus-area node. Keep a specific old content group tag for archived questions and review against the 2024 Data content before displaying as equivalent.
Direct match for continuous random variables and normal distribution. The 2024 node also includes discrete random variables from old S1.

implementation.replacementNotes: The 2024 syllabus starts Year 11 implementation in 2026, Year 12 implementation in Term 4 2026, with the first HSC exam in 2027.

Areas between curves and volumes of solids of revolution
Parametric form of a function or relation

Motion in vector form in two dimensions

nodes.title: The binomial distribution and sampling distribution of the mean

contentGroups.title: Sampling distribution of the mean and the central limit theorem

mappings.notes: Direct focus-area match for graphical relationships, inverse functions, parametric forms and inequalities.
Direct focus-area match for polynomial language, graphs, factor/remainder theorems and roots.
Questions focused on multiplicity and curve behaviour may display under the 2024 Further applications of calculus zeroes group.
The inverse trigonometric functions content moves from Year 11 in 2017 to Year 12 in 2024.
Further trigonometric identities remain in the 2024 Further trigonometry focus area.
Related rates and applied rate-of-change modelling align most closely with 2024 Further applications of calculus.
Routine derivative-skill questions from the 2017 Rates of Change subtopic can also display under 2024 Further calculus skills.
Permutation and combination content remains a Year 11 combinatorics focus area.
The 2017 binomial expansion and Pascal's triangle content is now separated as The binomial theorem.
Direct match for proof by mathematical induction.
Direct match for vector notation, operations, motion and projectiles.
2017 Year 12 trigonometric equations align with the 2024 Year 11 Further trigonometric equations group.
Direct match for further derivatives and integration techniques.
Direct match for multiplicity, rates, areas, volumes and differential equations.
The binomial distribution is retained and expanded with sampling distribution content in 2024.

implementation.replacementNotes: The 2024 syllabus starts implementation for Year 12 in Term 4 2026, with the first HSC exam in 2027.

The language and notation of proof

mappings.notes: Direct match for nature, notation and illustrations of proof, including inequalities.
Further induction is now a content group inside the 2024 The nature of proof focus area.
Direct match for vector equations and vector geometry.
Introductory complex-number arithmetic, geometry and representation remain in one 2024 focus area.
Using complex numbers is merged into the 2024 Introduction to complex numbers focus area.
Direct match for further integration techniques.
Direct match for mechanics, simple harmonic motion, resisted motion and projectiles.

implementation.replacementNotes: The 2024 syllabus starts Year 11 implementation in 2026, Year 12 implementation in Term 4 2026, with the first HSC exam in 2027.

mappings.notes: Formulae and Equations is renamed Formulas and equations in 2024.
Direct match for linear modelling and direct variation.
Direct Year 11 measurement match.
Working with Time is broadened to Time and location.
Money Matters splits into earning and managing money in 2024.
Money Matters splits into earning and managing money in 2024.
Direct data analysis match.
Relative frequency and probability moves into Year 12 Standard 1.
Relative frequency and probability moves into Year 12 Standard 2.
Standard 1 Types of Relationships becomes Algebraic relationships.
Direct match for right-angled triangles.
Standard 1 Rates is now grouped with ratios and rates.
Scale drawing questions should be reviewed against 2024 ratios and rates because there is no like-for-like focus area.
Direct Standard 1 investment match.
Direct Standard 1 depreciation and loans match.
Further statistical analysis maps primarily to Standard 1 bivariate data analysis; probability questions should use the relative-frequency mapping.
Use for Standard 1 further-statistics questions involving relative frequency or probability.
Standard 1 networks and paths content is now introduced in Year 11 Networks, paths and trees.
Standard 2 Types of Relationships becomes Algebraic relationships.
Non-right-angled Trigonometry is represented by the 2024 Standard 2 Trigonometry focus area.
Direct Standard 2 ratios and rates match.
Direct Standard 2 investment and loans match.
Direct Standard 2 annuities match.
Direct Standard 2 bivariate data analysis match.
Direct Standard 2 normal distribution match.
Use for foundational network-concept questions.
Use for network-flow questions under Standard 2.
Direct Standard 2 critical path analysis match.

guidance: Tag the syllabus version the paper was examined under as primary.
Resolve cross-syllabus display through mappings rather than duplicating tags manually.
When a question crosses topics, store multiple nodeIds and contentGroupIds.
For future non-Advanced courses, add displayable corpus syllabus nodes whose IDs match the conversion node IDs before importing exam questions.
Use mapping relationship and confidence to decide whether to show a direct equivalent, a related-content warning, or a reviewer note.
